description: Fix `opentabs config show --show-secret` to use the running server's actual port when generating MCP client config URLs, instead of reading from the config file (which may not reflect the `--port` flag or `OPENTABS_PORT` env var used at server start time).

userStories.title: config show --show-secret should resolve port consistently with the running server
userStories.description: As a plugin developer who started the server with `--port 9599`, I want `opentabs config show --show-secret` to print MCP client config URLs using port 9599 so that the config blocks I copy are correct.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs config show --show-secret --port 9599` prints MCP endpoint URLs with port 9599, not the default 9515
When the config file has no `port` field but `--port` is passed, the flag value is used for MCP URLs
When neither `--port` nor config `port` is set, the default 9515 is used (existing behavior preserved)
The port resolution follows the same priority as other commands: --port flag > OPENTABS_PORT env > config.port > DEFAULT_PORT
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, lines 202-204. Currently reads port as `typeof config.port === 'number' ? config.port : DEFAULT_PORT`. This ignores the `--port` flag and `OPENTABS_PORT` env var. The fix is to use `resolvePort(options)` from `platform/cli/src/parse-port.ts` (same function used by other commands) instead of reading config.port directly. The `resolvePort` function already implements the correct priority: --port flag > OPENTABS_PORT env > config.port > DEFAULT_PORT. The `options` object from Commander should already contain the `--port` value via the root command's option. Check that `handleConfigShow` receives the Commander options object with the port field.
